# SD&P assignment code repository checklist
<!-- This checklist is intended to help you ensure that your code repository meets the requirements for the SD&P assignment.-->
<!-- This is to be used as reference not a hard blocker. Feel free review each item and check it off if it applies to your PR -->

## README
- [ ] How to build/run the project.
- [ ] Link to Trello board.
- [ ] Brief project overview (a few sentences).
- [ ] Brief note on testing (e.g., "Unit tests are located in src/test/java").
- [ ] JavaDoc
  - [ ] A brief description of the purpose and responsibility of each class.
  - [ ] A brief description of what each public method does.
  - [ ] Explanations of parameters (@param) and return values (@return) where applicable.
  - [ ] Any important assumptions, constraints, or limitations.
  - [ ] A concise explanation of key design decisions where these help to justify the implementation approach.
  - [ ] JavaDoc comments should focus on explaining the intent of the code and any non-obvious implementation choices, rather than repeating what is already clear from the code itself.
  
## Coding Practice:
  - [ ] Consistent naming conventions
  - [ ] Appropriate decomposition into classes and methods.
  - [ ] Readability.
  - [ ] Use of JavaDoc and comments where appropriate.
  - [ ] Maintainable code structure.
